ip_outcome_note: suspected_ip was set to empty string — no actual IP to verify. Cleared as n/a.

trivia_bullets.text: Wastescape Battlemage is set on Zendikar, in the region of Tazeem, according to the MH3 art description documented in the Wizards 'Expanding Your Horizons: Eldrazi' design article. The 'wastescape' in the name references the desolate terrain left by the Eldrazi's consumption of the plane during Battle for Zendikar block [magic.wizards.com/en/news/making-magic/expanding-your-horizons-eldrazi].

The card is a deliberate callback to the Planeshift Battlemage cycle (2001) — a five-card cycle of monocolor 2/2 creatures each with two off-color kicker costs providing distinct ETB effects. The Wizards design article states the MH3 team aimed to capture 'enough of the feel of a Battlemage' through the dual kicker structure, where the {G} kicker exiles an artifact or enchantment and the {1}{U} kicker bounces a creature — mirroring the original cycle's removal/utility split [magic.wizards.com: Expanding Your Horizons: Eldrazi].

Wastescape Battlemage has the type line Eldrazi Wizard and costs {1}{C} — requiring colorless mana to cast. The {C} mana symbol, introduced in Oath of the Gatewatch (2016), is a design commitment: it cannot be paid by any colored mana, requiring Wastes or colorless-producing sources. This makes it a dedicated payoff for Eldrazi-tribal decks running colorless mana infrastructure. EDHREC rank 6372 — the strongest Commander adoption of this five-card batch [Scryfall type_line, mana_cost, EDHREC rank].

The card's dual kicker means it can answer both artifact/enchantment-based hate pieces and problematic creatures on the same turn when both kickers are paid — a flexible answer package on a body, echoing the 'Swiss Army knife' design intent of the original Planeshift Battlemages [magic.wizards.com: Expanding Your Horizons: Eldrazi; Planeshift design history via CoolStuffInc tour de cards article].
sources: coolstuffinc.com: Tour de Cards Planeshift and Apocalypse
